title: dryopteris immersa
intro: Dryopteris immersa, also known as autumn fern or creeping wood fern, is a type of evergreen fern that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is believed to have antiseptic, antiviral, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Dryopteris immersa include reducing inflammation, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting immune function, as well as being used to treat various skin conditions and wounds. Dryopteris immersa can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies, such as salves and creams, to promote healing and skin health.
